claim: The island variety showed a strong nocturnal nectar-volume pattern; the mainland variety changed less and had a lower reported maximum.

claim: The authors infer island var. platyphyllum is more dependent on crepuscular/nocturnal hawkmoths, whereas mainland var. auratum uses both diurnal swallowtails and nocturnal large hawkmoths.
notes: Alternative-pollinator mechanism context; This is a Lepidoptera-guild mechanism and must not be treated as staged Bombus-loss evidence.

claim: Nocturnally pollinated flowers had higher seed set than diurnally pollinated flowers (p<0.01).

comparison_units: Izu-Oshima/Aogashima var. platyphyllum and Hachioji var. auratum
